Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逾半载 - Means 'more than half a year,' indicating a period of time.
  • 刺史卸事回 - Means 'the governor returned after handing over his duties,' referring to the end of an official term.
  • 自作一札焚之 - Means 'he wrote a letter and burned it himself,' indicating a symbolic act.
  • 怪答以一帖 - Means 'the ghost replied with a note,' suggesting a supernatural response.
  • 非篆非草，不可辩 - Means 'neither seal script nor cursive, and cannot be deciphered,' describing an indecipherable script.
  • 倏然去 - Means 'disappeared suddenly,' indicating a sudden departure.
  • 二事皆得之传闻 - Means 'both events were heard through hearsay,' suggesting they are not firsthand accounts.
  • 惟张萱思家曾亲见之 - Means 'only Zhang Xuansi’s family had seen it personally,' indicating a rare firsthand witness.
  • 怪无形而有声 - Means 'the ghost was formless but made sounds,' describing a supernatural phenomenon.
  • 主人詈之为怪 - Means 'the owner cursed it as a ghost,' indicating a reaction to the supernatural event.
  • 恶声必反，皆市井秽语 - Means 'the ghost always replied with foul language, all vulgar street talk,' describing the ghost's behavior.
  • 亦不甚祟人 - Means 'it did not greatly harm people,' indicating limited malevolence.
  • 惟好作神会中所谓钱旛戏者 - Means 'but it liked to play the money banner game as in the spiritual gathering,' describing a specific supernatural activity.
  • 窃人烛台为之 - Means 'it stole people's candle stands to play,' indicating a mischievous act.
  • 或伺其戏击焉，空若无物 - Means 'someone would sometimes try to hit it when it was playing, but it was empty as if there was nothing,' describing an attempt to interact with the ghost.
  • 惟烛台愈高，且出簷外，终日不倦 - Means 'only the candle stand became higher and higher, even sticking out of the eaves, and it did not tire all day,' describing the ghost's persistent activity.
  • 后萱思迁去，空其室乃已 - Means 'After Zhang Xuansi moved away, the ghost stopped only when the room was empty,' indicating the ghost's departure.
  • 余戏谓班昭以九等论人 - Means 'I jokingly said that Ban Zhao would classify people into nine ranks,' introducing a humorous comparison.
  • 沈师楼上游戏三昧 - Means 'Shen Shi played the threefold meditation on the upper floor,' describing a spiritual practice.
  • 即闺中口耳之欲亦不以累人 - Means 'even the desires of the inner chambers did not burden people,' indicating a high level of spiritual attainment.
  • 至假托乩仙，敦诗说礼，不愧风流鬼魅也 - Means 'up to pretending to be a spirit medium, teaching poetry and propriety, it was not ashamed to be a charming ghost,' describing the ghost's sophisticated behavior.
  • 闽省邸中，因人弗顺而嬲之 - Means 'In the inn of Fujian Province, it annoyed people because they did not obey,' indicating the ghost's reaction to disobedience.
  • 能使闺襜侈泰，技无所施 - Means 'it could make the inner chambers extravagant and show off its skills,' describing the ghost's influence.
  • 且来去飘然，狡狯亦不恶 - Means 'and it came and went lightly, its cunning was not bad,' indicating the ghost's elusive and clever nature.
  • 至萱思家物怪之中又有伧父焉 - Means 'Among the strange objects in Zhang Xuansi’s family, there was also a coarse man,' introducing another supernatural entity.
  • 恐沈杜两家均不屑与之为伍矣 - Means 'Perhaps Shen and Du families would not associate with it,' suggesting a low regard for the coarse man.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刺史 - Refers to a governor, a high-ranking official in ancient China.
  • 札 - Refers to a letter or document.
  • 怪 - Refers to a ghost or supernatural entity.
  • 篆 - Refers to seal script, a type of Chinese writing.
  • 草 - Refers to cursive script, another type of Chinese writing.
  • 神会 - Refers to a spiritual gathering or meeting.
  • 钱旛戏 - Refers to a traditional Chinese game involving money banners.
  • 班昭 - A famous female scholar and writer of the Han Dynasty, known for her work on the *Book of Han*.
  • 沈师 - Refers to Shen Shi, a spiritual practitioner.
  • 三昧 - Refers to a state of deep meditation or concentration in Buddhist practice.
  • 闽省 - Refers to Fujian Province, a region in southeastern China.
  • 伧父 - Refers to a coarse or uncouth person.

Proper Nouns

  • 张萱思 - Refers to Zhang Xuansi, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 沈师 - Refers to Shen Shi, a spiritual practitioner.
  • 班昭 - Refers to Ban Zhao, a famous female scholar and writer of the Han Dynasty.
  • 闽省 - Refers to Fujian Province, a region in southeastern China.
  • 伧父 - Refers to a coarse or uncouth person.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惟乩上神仙络绎降灵 - Only the spirits of immortals descend in succession on the oracle board, indicating a series of divine or supernatural appearances.
  • 不啻黄九烟《将就园记》中语也 - No less than the words in Huang Jiuyan's *Jiangjiu Yuanji* (Record of the Compromising Garden), comparing the praise to a famous text.
  • 从此扶之不动矣 - From then on, the oracle board would not move, indicating a final change in behavior.
  • 或疑此即楼上人弄狡狯也 - Some suspect that this was the person upstairs playing tricks, suggesting a possible alternative explanation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乩上神仙 - Refers to spirits or immortals that communicate through an oracle board, a common practice in Chinese folk religion.
  • 云间先哲 - Refers to revered sages or philosophers from the heavens, often associated with Confucian and Taoist traditions.
  • 师曾祖 - Refers to the master's great-grandfather, emphasizing the importance of family lineage and respect for ancestors.
  • 《玉枢经》 - Refers to the *Yujuzhijing* (Pivot of the Jade Treasury), a Taoist scripture.
  • 《玉枢宝忏》 - Refers to a proposed supplementary text to the *Yujuzhijing*.
  • 文昌宫 - Refers to the Palace of Literature, a place associated with literary success and wisdom in Chinese folklore.
  • 黄九烟《将就园记》 - Refers to a famous literary work by Huang Jiuyan, known for its eloquent and persuasive style.
  • 忠孝 - Refers to the Confucian virtues of loyalty and filial piety.
  • 道义交 - Refers to a friendship based on moral principles.
  • 灵鬼 - Refers to spiritual ghosts, often seen as less noble than true immortals.

Proper Nouns

  • 《玉枢宝忏》 - A proposed supplementary text to the *Yujuzhijing*.
  • 文昌宫 - The Palace of Literature, a place associated with literary success and wisdom in Chinese folklore.
  • 黄九烟《将就园记》 - A famous literary work by Huang Jiuyan.

  • 一如 - Classical connective meaning 'just as' or 'exactly like,' used to introduce a comparison.
  • 逆伦 - Refers to 'violating ethical norms,' specifically in the context of familial and moral violations.
  • 天罗地网 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'a vast network of surveillance' or 'an inescapable trap.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 囚我于光中见之 - Cryptic phrase likely referring to a supernatural or mystical experience. '囚我' means 'imprisoned me,' and '于光中见之' means 'in the light, I saw it.'
  • 囚初逃至一处 - '初逃' means 'first escaped,' and '至一处' means 'to a place.'
  • 有中年妇赠以衣一领，钱五百 - '赠以' means 'gave,' '衣一领' means 'a piece of clothing,' and '钱五百' means 'five hundred coins.'
  • 复返其家 - '复返' means 'returned,' and '其家' means 'his/her home.'
  • 则一翁一妪呼两青衣执之矣 - '则' means 'then,' '一翁一妪' means 'an old man and an old woman,' '呼' means 'called,' '两青衣' means 'two attendants,' and '执之矣' means 'seized him/her.'
  • 今已十三 - '今已' means 'now has,' and '十三' means 'thirteen years old.'
  • 既望即就缚 - '既望' means 'the full moon,' and '就缚' means 'was bound.'
  • 我固知之 - '我固' means 'I indeed,' and '知之' means 'knew it.'
  • 爽则谪我 - '爽' means 'if I am wrong,' and '谪我' means 'punish me.'
  • 至院以告 - '至院' means 'to the court,' and '以告' means 'to report.'
  • 叶华又弄狡狯矣 - '又弄狡狯' means 'again played tricks.'
  • 叙州去省三百里 - '去省' means 'from the province,' and '三百里' means 'three hundred miles.'
  • 遣仆杨驰覘之 - '遣仆' means 'sent a servant,' '杨' is the servant's name, and '驰覘之' means 'to spy on him.'
  • 杨诨号八百里 - '诨号' means 'nickname,' and '八百里' means 'eight hundred miles.'
  • 十八日，余偕胡雪方至院 - '偕' means 'with,' '胡雪方' is a person's name, and '至院' means 'to the court.'
  • 与一如、肖廉共饭 - '共饭' means 'had a meal together.'
  • 饭后节相亦来共话 - '节相' is a title, and '共话' means 'to chat together.'
  • 忽肖廉仆汪福抚掌入曰 - '抚掌' means 'clapped hands,' and '入曰' means 'entered and said.'
  • 囚果擒矣 - '果擒' means 'was indeed captured.'
  • 八百里已款门 - '款门' means 'knocked on the door.'
  • 一如遽起讯问 - '遽起' means 'quickly stood up,' and '讯问' means 'to question.'
  • 逾阈触于楹，几仆 - '逾阈' means 'stepped over the threshold,' '触于楹' means 'touched the pillars,' and '几仆' means 'almost fell.'
  • 一如坦率，诚不如谢安石之镇定 - '坦率' means 'frank,' '诚不如' means 'indeed not as,' and '谢安石之镇定' refers to the historical figure Xie An, known for his composure.
  • 咸就杨细诘叶语 - '咸' means 'all,' '就杨' means 'to Yang,' and '细诘' means 'carefully questioned.'
  • 一一吻合 - '吻合' means 'matched perfectly.'
  • 中年妇其姊 - '其姊' means 'her sister.'
  • 翁妪即其父母 - '即' means 'were,' and '其父母' means 'his/her parents.'
  • 恶囚杀叔，且贪赏 - '恶囚杀叔' means 'hated the prisoner for killing his uncle,' and '且贪赏' means 'and coveted the reward.'
  • 故诱之饮醉，呼里正缚之也 - '故诱之' means 'so they lured him,' '饮醉' means 'to drink him drunk,' '呼里正' means 'called the village head,' and '缚之' means 'bound him.'
  • 与茸城所见老生术正同 - '茸城' is a place name, '老生术' means 'old scholar's method,' and '正同' means 'the same as.'
  • 至于鲁都游都及天罗地网等 - '鲁都游都' and '天罗地网' are likely references to specific techniques or strategies.
  • 沈丹彩曾言之 - '沈丹彩' is a person's name, and '曾言之' means 'once mentioned it.'
  • 似非叶华所及 - '似非' means 'seems not,' and '叶华所及' means 'within the reach of Ye Hua.'
  • 特假以神其说 - '特假以' means 'especially by,' and '神其说' means 'to make his words seem magical.'
  • 一如酬表里一，金三十 - '酬表里' means 'rewarded inside and out,' and '金三十' means 'thirty coins.'
  • 视茸城老生，真成洴澼洸矣 - '视' means 'compared to,' '茸城老生' refers to an old scholar from Rongcheng, and '洴澼洸' is a metaphor for being useless or ineffective.
  • 后叶军前效力，竟坐妖言惑众，发新疆 - '后' means 'later,' '军前效力' means 'served in the army,' '竟坐妖言惑众' means 'was ultimately punished for spreading false rumors,' and '发新疆' means 'sent to Xinjiang.'

  • 中年妇 - Refers to a middle-aged woman.
  • 翁妪 - Refers to an old man and an old woman.
